stem: An engineer takes 20 measurements of room temperature using a thermometer. The measurements have a mean of 132.4 °C and a standard deviation of 16.2 °C. However, calibration tests reveal that a thermometer systematically underestimates the true value by between 1.7% and 5.6%. Estimate the true room temperature and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 132.4 °C. After correcting for the 3.6% bias: ≈ 137.3 °C. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (1.7%-5.6%) and sampling variability: [127.5, 147.5]. Point estimate: 137.3 °C.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
